methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Cover, a Series B-stage company in Housing & Construction founded by Alexis Rivas, has raised over $100M to date. The Housing & Construction sector shows limited comparable company depth, with only one identified private peer (ICON) and no public comparables, constraining traditional multiples-based approaches.

Stage-progression methodology applies here, using the $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups in frontier tech. This yields an implied valuation of $190M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the Housing & Construction sector; a single peer does not support robust multiples triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M, with a median point of $190M. Key uncertainties include the limited peer set (one private comparable, zero public), which constrains validation of sector-specific multiples and stage-step assumptions. The reliance on stage-progression methodology as the sole applicable approach introduces dependence on historical step-up norms that may not reflect Housing & Construction-specific capital dynamics or Cover's specific competitive position. Additional comparable data or revenue/EBITDA multiples would strengthen conviction in this range.
